Capturing active demand, and it tends to be easier to model and just start to scale.
But there's a natural headroom there.
There's diminishing returns.
There's going to be things that if you rely on a single channel for the growth of your company, that is not a great place to be.
Like, any things that are out of your control can start to affect you.
And then as you need to hit that next level of growth, you have limited levers to be able to do that.
So a lot of times companies would hire me because they're stuck on that problem.
How do you actually get more repeatable channels outside of paid search?

So, you know, taking that back to what I was doing at Homebase, I had previous to working with GrowthX, I had a whole set of different freelancers, I had a pretty sizable budget for organic, I had a few people internally, it was just a lot of different coordination and time and attention effort to start to drive that growth.

We were able to drive a lot of growth, it was just really, it took a lot of time, it was very expensive, I had a whole bunch of other things I had to do completely outside of that, standing up other channels, so it was like really hard to be able to do something that requires that much attention and do.
So we were getting some momentum, but certainly the cost of what all that came to, the speed, I just felt that equation could have been a bit more right-sized.

The thing to keep in mind with where programmatic oftentimes takes shape is usually you're going after something that there is a what is sometimes called a head term, in this case per diem being the head term, and that there's a lot of variance of what people are searching associated to that head term.
So per diem is essentially, if you're not familiar, like there's these different per diem rates for people who are doing business travel that are set at city and state levels.
I admittedly don't understand it at super deep level, but like largely that's kind of what it is.
